body.title: Inference routing is a cost-optimizing selector over rich data — two categorical bucket-filters + argmin(dollars), NOT a hardcoded cascade
body.text:
# D-inference-cost-optimizing-router-2026-06-30
**title:** Inference routing = cost-optimizing selector over rich data (two bucket-filters + argmin dollars), not a hardcoded cascade
**date:** 2026-06-30
**status:** open
**spawned_tickets:** T-router-data-schema, T-router-selector, T-ds-local-ollama-route, T-router-live-resource-read, T-consequence-D-inference-cost-optimizing-router

## Akien's framing (verbatim intent)
HARD AGREE — 'this was the intention all along. This is what I was trying to build. We finally have it right.' This epic is the convergence point of the compiled-inference vision, the tier-not-model contract, and inference-proxy-only: the router Akien has been building toward across the whole project.

## Decision narrative
The Inference Proxy NEVER says 'this model then this model then this model.' Per call it asks ONE question — 'where is the lowest cost, in dollars AND time, to run THIS call given its estimated difficulty and required features?' — and picks the argmin over whatever sources CAN do it. The rule stays trivial; ALL richness lives in the per-source DATA. Both design-time open params resolved as CATEGORICAL BUCKETS, not continuous weights:
- TIME is an eligibility filter: buckets = interactive-seconds / minutes / overnight; the call's URGENCY picks the eligible bucket; a free-but-slow source is simply NOT a candidate for interactive work (re-enters only for overnight). Buckets are LIVE, not static labels — Hex collapsed a bucket (an 8b job that needed a >5-min timeout on akiensyoga9i now runs in seconds), so the optimizer must READ live per-source latency or it re-exiles Hex.
- DIFFICULTY is a capability filter: buckets = classify < code < design; the call's TASK-CLASS already IS the bucket (minion/worker/designer), so a-priori difficulty needs zero new machinery. Exactness is unnecessary because MECHANICAL-failure escalation bumps the difficulty bucket one rung and re-runs the SAME selector — one mechanism, dissolving the availability/capability escalation fork. Graph-tree falls out of the floor: classify is so cheap it shouldn't hit an LLM at all (cost_rank 0 / compiled inference).
FINAL SHAPE: filter by (time_bucket eligible) x (difficulty_capable >= call bucket) x (features cover) -> argmin(dollars) -> on mechanical failure bump difficulty and re-run (terminates cleanly past the top bucket). Hex-DOWN is not a branch: an unavailable source is just absent from candidates -> next-cheapest wins; the down raises a separate system_alarm. Providers are open-ended (OR, ollama-local, ollama-cloud, anthropic, huggingface, 'a zillion more' + user combos) — the RULES must not grow to cover them; the DATA MODEL must be rich enough that the same selector keeps working as providers are added.

## Decomposition (EPIC -> data-increments)
(1) T-router-data-schema — the per-source descriptor {time_bucket, difficulty_capable, dollars_per_unit, features} + per-call mappers (urgency->time-bucket, task_class->difficulty-bucket) + crossing-log fields. Schema only, no selector.
(2) T-router-selector — the trivial selector: two bucket-filters -> argmin(dollars); mechanical-failure difficulty-bump re-run (quality-judge deferred; terminates past top bucket); retire binary billing_type + foreground inversion + dead local_ollama.
(3) T-ds-local-ollama-route (reshaped) — register Hex as the first fully-described source (dollars=0 owned-local, measured interactive time_bucket, per-tier difficulty), INFERENCE_ENDPOINT->Hex, DS drops model_override->worker tier. Supersedes the static cost-rank ladder.
(4) T-router-live-resource-read — LIVE re-measurement so time_buckets never go stale (observed latency -> rolling window -> recompute bucket -> write back -> log transition); also sheds load off an overloaded-but-up source. The headline principle; time_bucket's missing corrector.

## Hypothesis
After these ship, inference routing picks the cheapest capable source per call by a trivial rule over data: a worker/DS call lands on Hex (free, interactive) instead of cloud when Hex is up and fast enough, cloud appears only on escalation or Hex-down, and adding a new provider is a data edit with ZERO edits to the selector.

## Measurement Signal
The routing crossing-log line names the chosen source + its time/difficulty bucket + dollars; grep shows Hex winning interactive worker calls and cloud only on escalation/Hex-down. Adding a source = editing one data file, no selector code change. (Akien: 'we can just look at the logs on a ticket.')

## Intention
I intend that inference routing picks the cheapest capable source per call automatically, so adding a new provider is a data entry, never a code change.
